message.content: Looking at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/issues/739 would it make sense to have a "brain" option next to the provider/llm in the web-ui, enabled when the model has a thinking mode (low/medium/high etc)? How many models do we support which have thinking mode, with different thinking mode?

content.text: Let me look at the issue and explore the codebase to understand the current thinking mode support.

input.prompt:
Very thorough exploration: Find all code related to "thinking" mode, "reasoning" mode, "extended thinking", "reasoning_effort", "thinking_budget", "budget_tokens" in the codebase. I need to understand:

1. Which providers/models support thinking mode
2. How thinking mode is currently configured and passed through
3. What different thinking levels/modes exist (low/medium/high etc.)
4. How ModelInfo or similar structs track thinking capability
5. Any existing UI support for thinking mode

Search across all crates, especially providers, agents, config, gateway, and web UI assets.

    68→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
    69→struct UsageFields {
    70→    input_tokens: u32,
    71→    output_tokens: u32,
    72→    cache_read_tokens: u32,
    73→    cache_write_tokens: u32,
    74→}
    75→
    76→impl From<&Usage> for UsageFields {
    77→    fn from(usage: &Usage) -> Self {
    78→        Self {
    79→            input_tokens: usage.input_tokens,
    80→            output_tokens: usage.output_tokens,
    81→            cache_read_tokens: usage.cache_read_tokens,
    82→            cache_write_tokens: usage.cache_write_tokens,
    83→        }
    84→    }
    85→}
    86→
    87→impl UsageSnapshot {
    88→    #[must_use]
    89→    pub(crate) fn new(total: Usage, request: Option<Usage>) -> Self {
    90→        Self { total, request }
    91→    }
    92→
    93→    fn total_fields(&self) -> UsageFields {
    94→        UsageFields::from(&self.total)
    95→    }
    96→
    97→    fn request_fields(&self) -> Option<UsageFields> {
    98→        self.request.as_ref().map(UsageFields::from)
    99→    }
   100→
   101→    fn request_or_total_fields(&self) -> UsageFields {
   102→        self.request_fields().unwrap_or_else(|| self.total_fields())
   103→    }
   104→}

   174→#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
   175→pub(crate) fn build_chat_final_broadcast(
   176→    run_id: &str,
   177→    session_key: &str,
   178→    text: String,
   179→    model: String,
   180→    provider: String,
   181→    usage: UsageSnapshot,
   182→    duration_ms: u64,
   183→    message_index: usize,
   184→    reply_medium: ReplyMedium,
   185→    iterations: Option<usize>,
   186→    tool_calls_made: Option<usize>,
   187→    audio: Option<String>,
   188→    audio_warning: Option<String>,
   189→    reasoning: Option<String>,
   190→    seq: Option<u64>,
   191→) -> ChatFinalBroadcast {
   192→    let total = usage.total_fields();
   193→    let request = usage.request_fields();
   194→    ChatFinalBroadcast {
   195→        run_id: run_id.to_string(),
   196→        session_key: session_key.to_string(),
   197→        state: "final",
   198→        text,
   199→        model,
   200→        provider,
   201→        input_tokens: total.input_tokens,
   202→        output_tokens: total.output_tokens,
   203→        cache_read_tokens: total.cache_read_tokens,
   204→        cache_write_tokens: total.cache_write_tokens,
   205→        duration_ms,
   206→        request_input_tokens: request.map(|usage| usage.input_tokens),
   207→        request_output_tokens: request.map(|usage| usage.output_tokens),
   208→        request_cache_read_tokens: request.map(|usage| usage.cache_read_tokens),
   209→        request_cache_write_tokens: request.map(|usage| usage.cache_write_tokens),
   210→        message_index,
   211→        reply_medium,
   212→        iterations,
   213→        tool_calls_made,
   214→        audio,
   215→        audio_warning,
   216→        reasoning,
   217→        seq,
   218→    }
   219→}
   220→
   221→pub(crate) fn build_assistant_turn_output(
   222→    text: String,
   223→    usage: UsageSnapshot,
   224→    duration_ms: u64,
   225→    audio_path: Option<String>,
   226→    reasoning: Option<String>,
   227→    llm_api_response: Option<Value>,
   228→) -> AssistantTurnOutput {
   229→    let total = usage.total_fields();
   230→    let request = usage.request_or_total_fields();
   231→    AssistantTurnOutput {
   232→        text,

   234→        output_tokens: total.output_tokens,
   235→        cache_read_tokens: total.cache_read_tokens,
   236→        cache_write_tokens: total.cache_write_tokens,
   237→        duration_ms,
   238→        request_input_tokens: request.input_tokens,
   239→        request_output_tokens: request.output_tokens,
   240→        request_cache_read_tokens: request.cache_read_tokens,
   241→        request_cache_write_tokens: request.cache_write_tokens,
   242→        audio_path,
   243→        reasoning,
   244→        llm_api_response,
   245→    }
   246→}

   248→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
   249→pub(crate) struct SessionTokenUsage {
   250→    pub session_input_tokens: u64,
   251→    pub session_output_tokens: u64,
   252→    pub session_cache_read_tokens: u64,
   253→    pub session_cache_write_tokens: u64,
   254→    pub current_request_input_tokens: u64,
   255→    pub current_request_output_tokens: u64,
   256→    pub current_request_cache_read_tokens: u64,
   257→    pub current_request_cache_write_tokens: u64,
   258→}
   259→
   260→#[must_use]
   261→pub(crate) fn session_token_usage_from_messages(messages: &[Value]) -> SessionTokenUsage {
   262→    let session_input_tokens = messages
   263→        .iter()
   264→        .filter_map(|m| m.get("inputTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   265→        .sum();
   266→    let session_output_tokens = messages
   267→        .iter()
   268→        .filter_map(|m| m.get("outputTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   269→        .sum();
   270→    let session_cache_read_tokens = messages
   271→        .iter()
   272→        .filter_map(|m| m.get("cacheReadTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   273→        .sum();
   274→    let session_cache_write_tokens = messages
   275→        .iter()
   276→        .filter_map(|m| m.get("cacheWriteTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   277→        .sum();
   278→
   279→    let (
   280→        current_request_input_tokens,
   281→        current_request_output_tokens,
   282→        current_request_cache_read_tokens,
   283→        current_request_cache_write_tokens,
   284→    ) = messages
   285→        .iter()
   286→        .rev()
   287→        .find(|m| m.get("role").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some("assistant"))
   288→        .map_or((0, 0, 0, 0), |m| {
   289→            let input = m
   290→                .get("requestInputTokens")
   291→                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
   292→                .or_else(|| m.get("inputTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   293→                .unwrap_or(0);
   294→            let output = m
   295→                .get("requestOutputTokens")
   296→                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
   297→                .or_else(|| m.get("outputTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   298→                .unwrap_or(0);
   299→            let cache_read = m
   300→                .get("requestCacheReadTokens")
   301→                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
   302→                .or_else(|| m.get("cacheReadTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   303→                .unwrap_or(0);
   304→            let cache_write = m
   305→                .get("requestCacheWriteTokens")
   306→                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
   307→                .or_else(|| m.get("cacheWriteTokens").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))
   308→                .unwrap_or(0);
   309→            (input, output, cache_read, cache_write)
   310→        });
   311→
   312→    SessionTokenUsage {
   313→        session_input_tokens,
   314→        session_output_tokens,
   315→        session_cache_read_tokens,
   316→        session_cache_write_tokens,
   317→        current_request_input_tokens,
   318→        current_request_output_tokens,
   319→        current_request_cache_read_tokens,
   320→        current_request_cache_write_tokens,
   321→    }
   322→}
   323→
   324→#[cfg(test)]
   325→mod tests {
   326→    use {
   327→        super::{
   328→            ReplyMedium, UsageSnapshot, build_assistant_turn_output, build_chat_final_broadcast,
   329→            session_token_usage_from_messages,
   330→        },
   331→        moltis_agents::model::Usage,
   332→    };
   333→
   334→    #[test]
   335→    fn session_token_usage_tracks_cached_tokens() {
   336→        let messages = vec![
   337→            serde_json::json!({
   338→                "role": "assistant",
   339→                "inputTokens": 200,
   340→                "outputTokens": 20,
   341→                "cacheReadTokens": 150,
   342→                "cacheWriteTokens": 10,
   343→                "requestInputTokens": 180,
   344→                "requestOutputTokens": 18,
   345→                "requestCacheReadTokens": 140,
   346→                "requestCacheWriteTokens": 8
   347→            }),
   348→            serde_json::json!({
   349→                "role": "assistant",
   350→                "inputTokens": 300,
   351→                "outputTokens": 30,
   352→                "cacheReadTokens": 120,
   353→                "cacheWriteTokens": 5,
   354→                "requestInputTokens": 250,
   355→                "requestOutputTokens": 25,
   356→                "requestCacheReadTokens": 100,
   357→                "requestCacheWriteTokens": 2
   358→            }),
   359→        ];
   360→
   361→        let usage = session_token_usage_from_messages(&messages);
   362→
   363→        assert_eq!(usage.session_input_tokens, 500);
   364→        assert_eq!(usage.session_output_tokens, 50);
   365→        assert_eq!(usage.session_cache_read_tokens, 270);
   366→        assert_eq!(usage.session_cache_write_tokens, 15);
   367→        assert_eq!(usage.current_request_input_tokens, 250);
   368→        assert_eq!(usage.current_request_output_tokens, 25);
   369→        assert_eq!(usage.current_request_cache_read_tokens, 100);
   370→        assert_eq!(usage.current_request_cache_write_tokens, 2);
   371→    }
   372→
   373→    #[test]
   374→    fn build_chat_final_broadcast_includes_cache_usage() {
   375→        let usage = Usage {

   377→            output_tokens: 80,
   378→            cache_read_tokens: 1050,
   379→            cache_write_tokens: 4,
   380→        };
   381→        let request_usage = Usage {
   382→            input_tokens: 900,

   384→            cache_read_tokens: 850,
   385→            cache_write_tokens: 2,
   386→        };
   387→
   388→        let payload = build_chat_final_broadcast(
   389→            "run-1",
   390→            "main",
   391→            "hello".to_string(),
   392→            "gpt-4.1".to_string(),
   393→            "openai".to_string(),
   394→            UsageSnapshot::new(usage, Some(request_usage)),
   395→            250,
   396→            7,

   405→
   406→        assert_eq!(payload.cache_read_tokens, 1050);

   445→#[must_use]
   446→pub(crate) fn assistant_message_is_visible(message: &Value) -> bool {
   447→    if message.get("role").and_then(Value::as_str) != Some("assistant") {
   448→        return true;
   449→    }
   450→
   451→    ["content", "reasoning"].iter().any(|field| {
   452→        message
   453→            .get(*field)
   454→            .and_then(Value::as_str)
   455→            .is_some_and(|text| !text.trim().is_empty())
   456→    })
   457→}

   459→#[must_use]
   460→pub(crate) fn estimate_text_tokens(text: &str) -> u64 {
   461→    let trimmed = text.trim();
   462→    if trimmed.is_empty() {
   463→        return 0;
   464→    }
   465→    let bytes = trimmed.len() as u64;
   466→    bytes.div_ceil(4).max(1)
   467→}
   468→
   469→/// Compute the auto-compact trigger threshold for a given context window
   470→/// and user-configured `chat.compaction.threshold_percent`.
   471→///
   472→/// The returned value is the number of estimated next-request input
   473→/// tokens at or above which `send()` fires a pre-emptive compaction.
   474→/// The fraction is clamped to `[0.1, 0.95]` so a typo'd config can't
   475→/// disable auto-compact or spam it on every message, and the result is
   476→/// floored at `1` so zero-context windows still get a non-zero check.
   477→#[must_use]
   478→pub(crate) fn compute_auto_compact_threshold(
   479→    context_window_tokens: u64,
   480→    threshold_percent: f32,
   481→) -> u64 {
   482→    let fraction = f64::from(threshold_percent.clamp(0.1, 0.95));
   483→    ((context_window_tokens as f64) * fraction).round().max(1.0) as u64
   484→}

   486→pub(crate) fn now_ms() -> u64 {
   487→    std::time::SystemTime::now()
   488→        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
   489→        .unwrap_or_default()
   490→        .as_millis() as u64
   491→}

   494→pub(crate) fn truncate_at_char_boundary(text: &str, max_bytes: usize) -> &str {
   495→    &text[..text.floor_char_boundary(max_bytes)]
   496→}
   497→
   498→/// Extract preview text from a single message JSON value.
   499→pub(crate) fn extract_preview_from_value(msg: &Value) -> Option<String> {
   500→    fn message_text(msg: &Value) -> Option<String> {
   501→        let text = if let Some(s) = msg.get("content").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
   502→            s.to_string()
   503→        } else if let Some(blocks) = msg.get("content").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
   504→            blocks
   505→                .iter()
   506→                .filter_map(|b| {
   507→                    if b.get("type").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some("text") {
   508→                        b.get("text").and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   509→                    } else {
   510→                        None
   511→                    }
   512→                })
   513→                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
   514→                .join(" ")
   515→        } else {
   516→            return None;
   517→        };
   518→        let trimmed = text.trim();
   519→        if trimmed.is_empty() {
   520→            None
   521→        } else {
   522→            Some(trimmed.to_string())
   523→        }
   524→    }
   525→    fn truncate_preview(s: &str, max: usize) -> String {
   526→        if s.len() <= max {
   527→            s.to_string()
   528→        } else {
   529→            format!("{}…", &s[..s.floor_char_boundary(max)])
   530→        }
   531→    }
   532→    message_text(msg).map(|t| truncate_preview(&t, 200))
   533→}
   534→
   535→/// Maximum total characters for a compaction summary.
   536→pub(crate) const SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS: usize = 1_200;
   537→/// Maximum number of lines in a compaction summary (excluding omission notice).
   538→pub(crate) const SUMMARY_MAX_LINES: usize = 24;
   539→/// Maximum characters per line in a compaction summary.
   540→pub(crate) const SUMMARY_MAX_LINE_CHARS: usize = 160;
   541→
   542→/// Compress a compaction summary to fit within budget constraints.
   543→///
   544→/// Enforces: max 1,200 chars total, max 24 lines, max 160 chars per line.
   545→/// Deduplicates lines (case-insensitive), preserves headers and bullets,
   546→/// and appends an omission notice when lines are dropped.
   547→#[must_use]
   548→pub(crate) fn compress_summary(text: &str) -> String {
   549→    let text = text.trim();
   550→    if text.is_empty() {
   551→        return String::new();
   552→    }
   553→
   554→    let lines: Vec<&str> = text.lines().collect();
   555→    if lines.is_empty() {
   556→        return String::new();
   557→    }
   558→
   559→    // Step 1: deduplicate lines (case-insensitive, keep first occurrence)
   560→    // and strip blank lines so they don't consume the 24-line budget.
   561→    let mut seen = HashSet::new();
   562→    let mut deduped: Vec<String> = Vec::with_capacity(lines.len());
   563→    for line in lines {
   564→        let key = line.trim().to_ascii_lowercase();
   565→        // Drop blank lines — they waste budget without adding content.
   566→        if key.is_empty() {
   567→            continue;
   568→        }
   569→        if seen.insert(key) {
   570→            deduped.push(if line.len() <= SUMMARY_MAX_LINE_CHARS {
   571→                line.to_string()
   572→            } else {
   573→                // Step 2: truncate individual lines exceeding 160 chars.
   574→                line[..line.floor_char_boundary(SUMMARY_MAX_LINE_CHARS)].to_string()
   575→            });
   576→        }
   577→    }
   578→    drop(seen);
   579→
   580→    // Step 3: check if already within budget.
   581→    let joined = deduped.join("\n");
   582→    if deduped.len() <= SUMMARY_MAX_LINES && joined.len() <= SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS {
   583→        return joined;
   584→    }
   585→
   586→    // Step 4: priority-based line dropping.
   587→    // Headers (starting with #) get highest priority, then bullets (- * •), then rest.
   588→    fn is_header(line: &str) -> bool {
   589→        line.trim_start().starts_with('#')
   590→    }
   591→    fn is_bullet(line: &str) -> bool {
   592→        let trimmed = line.trim_start();
   593→        trimmed.starts_with("- ") || trimmed.starts_with("* ") || trimmed.starts_with("• ")
   594→    }
   595→
   596→    let mut headers: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   597→    let mut bullet_lines: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   598→    let mut other_lines: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   599→
   600→    for line in deduped {
   601→        if is_header(&line) {
   602→            headers.push(line);
   603→        } else if is_bullet(&line) {
   604→            bullet_lines.push(line);
   605→        } else {
   606→            other_lines.push(line);
   607→        }
   608→    }
   609→
   610→    // Build ordered candidate list: bullets first, then others.
   611→    // Headers are always kept.
   612→    let mut candidates: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   613→    candidates.extend(bullet_lines);
   614→    candidates.extend(other_lines);
   615→
   616→    let header_count = headers.len();
   617→
   618→    // Check if keeping all candidates fits.
   619→    if header_count + candidates.len() <= SUMMARY_MAX_LINES {
   620→        let total_len = headers.iter().chain(candidates.iter()).fold(0, |acc, l| {
   621→            acc + l.len() + 1 // +1 for newline
   622→        })
   623→        // fold overcounts by 1 (N newlines vs N-1 for join); subtract to correct.
   624→        .saturating_sub(1);
   625→        if total_len <= SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS {
   626→            let mut result = headers;
   627→            result.extend(candidates);
   628→            return result.join("\n");
   629→        }
   630→    }
   631→
   632→    // Need to drop lines from the end of candidates.
   633→    // Account for omission notice in budget.
   634→    fn make_notice(n: usize) -> String {
   635→        format!("[... {n} lines omitted for brevity]")
   636→    }
   637→
   638→    for drop_count in 1..=candidates.len() {
   639→        let keep_count = candidates.len() - drop_count;
   640→        let line_count = header_count + keep_count + 1; // +1 for omission notice
   641→        if line_count > SUMMARY_MAX_LINES {
   642→            continue;
   643→        }
   644→
   645→        let notice = make_notice(drop_count);
   646→        let kept_candidates = &candidates[..keep_count];
   647→        let total_len = headers
   648→            .iter()
   649→            .chain(kept_candidates.iter())
   650→            .fold(0, |acc, l| acc + l.len() + 1)
   651→            // fold overcounts by 1 (N newlines vs N-1 for join); subtract to correct.
   652→            .saturating_sub(1)
   653→            + notice.len()
   654→            + 1; // +1 for newline before notice
   655→
   656→        if total_len <= SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS {
   657→            let mut result = headers;
   658→            result.extend(kept_candidates.iter().cloned());
   659→            result.push(notice);
   660→            return result.join("\n");
   661→        }
   662→    }
   663→
   664→    // Edge case: even dropping all candidates, headers alone are too long.
   665→    // Force-truncate headers from the end.  Run two passes so the notice
   666→    // length is exact: first pass counts dropped headers, second pass
   667→    // builds the result with the correct budget.
   668→    let base_dropped = candidates.len();
   669→    let mut header_drop_count = 0usize;
   670→    {
   671→        // First pass: determine how many headers must be dropped.
   672→        let mut budget = SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS.saturating_sub(make_notice(base_dropped).len() + 1);
   673→        let mut kept = 0usize;
   674→        for line in &headers {
   675→            let needed = line.len()
   676→                + if kept == 0 {
   677→                    0
   678→                } else {
   679→                    1
   680→                };
   681→            if needed > budget || kept + 1 >= SUMMARY_MAX_LINES {
   682→                header_drop_count += 1;
   683→            } else {
   684→                budget -= needed;
   685→                kept += 1;
   686→            }
   687→        }
   688→    }
   689→
   690→    let notice = make_notice(base_dropped + header_drop_count);
   691→    // Second pass: rebuild with exact budget including final notice length.
   692→    let mut char_budget = SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS.saturating_sub(notice.len() + 1);
   693→    let mut result: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   694→    for line in &headers {
   695→        let needed = line.len()
   696→            + if result.is_empty() {
   697→                0
   698→            } else {
   699→                1
   700→            };
   701→        if needed > char_budget || result.len() + 1 >= SUMMARY_MAX_LINES {
   702→            continue;
   703→        }
   704→        char_budget -= needed;
   705→        result.push(line.clone());
   706→    }
   707→    result.push(notice);
   708→    result.join("\n")
   709→}
   710→
   711→/// Apply [`compress_summary`] to any `[Conversation Summary]` or
   712→/// `[Conversation Compacted]` message in a compacted history.
   713→///
   714→/// Walks each message, detects the summary prefix, compresses the body, and
   715→/// replaces the content in place. Non-summary messages are passed through
   716→/// unchanged, preserving the head/tail structure of modes like
   717→/// `recency_preserving`.
   718→pub(crate) fn compress_summary_in_history(mut history: Vec<Value>) -> Vec<Value> {
   719→    for msg in &mut history {
   720→        let Some(content) = msg.get("content").and_then(Value::as_str).map(String::from) else {
   721→            continue;
   722→        };
   723→        for prefix in ["[Conversation Summary]\n\n", "[Conversation Compacted]\n\n"] {
   724→            if let Some(body) = content.strip_prefix(prefix) {
   725→                let compressed = compress_summary(body);
   726→                if compressed.len() < body.len()
   727→                    && let Some(obj) = msg.as_object_mut()
   728→                {
   729→                    obj.insert(
   730→                        "content".into(),
   731→                        Value::String(format!("{prefix}{compressed}")),
   732→                    );
   733→                }
   734→                break;
   735→            }
   736→        }
   737→    }
   738→    history
   739→}
   740→
   741→pub(crate) fn shell_reply_text_from_exec_result(result: &Value) -> String {
   742→    let stdout = result
   743→        .get("stdout")
   744→        .and_then(Value::as_str)
   745→        .map(str::trim_end)
   746→        .unwrap_or("");
   747→    if !stdout.is_empty() {
   748→        return stdout.to_string();
   749→    }
   750→
   751→    let stderr = result
   752→        .get("stderr")
   753→        .and_then(Value::as_str)
   754→        .map(str::trim_end)
   755→        .unwrap_or("");
   756→    if !stderr.is_empty() {
   757→        return stderr.to_string();
   758→    }
   759→
   760→    let exit_code = result.get("exit_code").and_then(Value::as_i64).or_else(|| {
   761→        result
   762→            .get("exit_code")
   763→            .and_then(Value::as_u64)
   764→            .and_then(|code| i64::try_from(code).ok())
   765→    });
   766→    match exit_code {
   767→        Some(code) if code != 0 => format!("Command failed (exit {code})."),
   768→        _ => String::new(),
   769→    }
   770→}
   771→
   772→pub(crate) fn capped_tool_result_payload(result: &Value, max_len: usize) -> Value {
   773→    let mut capped = result.clone();
   774→    for field in &["stdout", "stderr"] {
   775→        if let Some(text) = capped.get(*field).and_then(Value::as_str)
   776→            && text.len() > max_len
   777→        {
   778→            let truncated = format!(
   779→                "{}\n\n... [truncated — {} bytes total]",
   780→                truncate_at_char_boundary(text, max_len),
   781→                text.len()
   782→            );
   783→            capped[*field] = Value::String(truncated);
   784→        }
   785→    }
   786→    capped
   787→}

   789→pub fn normalize_model_key(value: &str) -> String {
   790→    let mut normalized = String::with_capacity(value.len());
   791→    let mut last_was_separator = true;
   792→
   793→    for ch in value.trim().chars() {
   794→        if ch.is_ascii_alphanumeric() {
   795→            normalized.push(ch.to_ascii_lowercase());
   796→            last_was_separator = false;
   797→            continue;
   798→        }
   799→
   800→        if !last_was_separator {
   801→            normalized.push(' ');
   802→            last_was_separator = true;
   803→        }
   804→    }
   805→
   806→    normalized.trim().to_string()
   807→}

   828→/// Returns `true` if the model matches the allowlist patterns.
   829→/// An empty pattern list means all models are allowed.
   830→/// Matching is case-insensitive against the full model ID, raw model ID, and
   831→/// display name:
   832→/// - patterns with digits use exact-or-suffix matching (boundary aware)
   833→/// - patterns without digits use substring matching
   834→///
   835→/// This keeps precise model pins like "gpt 5.2" from matching variants such as
   836→/// "gpt-5.2-chat-latest", while still allowing broad buckets like "mini".
   837→#[allow(dead_code)]
   838→pub(crate) fn allowlist_pattern_matches_key(pattern: &str, key: &str) -> bool {
   839→    if pattern.chars().any(|ch| ch.is_ascii_digit()) {
   840→        if key == pattern {
   841→            return true;
   842→        }
   843→        return key
   844→            .strip_suffix(pattern)
   845→            .is_some_and(|prefix| prefix.ends_with(' '));
   846→    }
   847→    key.contains(pattern)
   848→}
   849→
   850→#[allow(dead_code)]
   851→pub fn model_matches_allowlist(model: &moltis_providers::ModelInfo, patterns: &[String]) -> bool {
   852→    if patterns.is_empty() {
   853→        return true;
   854→    }
   855→    if is_allowlist_exempt_provider(&model.provider) {
   856→        return true;
   857→    }
   858→    let full = normalize_model_key(&model.id);
   859→    let raw = normalize_model_key(moltis_providers::model_id::raw_model_id(&model.id));
   860→    let display = normalize_model_key(&model.display_name);
   861→    patterns.iter().any(|p| {
   862→        allowlist_pattern_matches_key(p, &full)
   863→            || allowlist_pattern_matches_key(p, &raw)
   864→            || allowlist_pattern_matches_key(p, &display)
   865→    })
   866→}
   867→
   868→#[allow(dead_code)]
   869→pub fn model_matches_allowlist_with_provider(
   870→    model: &moltis_providers::ModelInfo,
   871→    provider_name: Option<&str>,
   872→    patterns: &[String],
   873→) -> bool {
   874→    if provider_name.is_some_and(is_allowlist_exempt_provider) {
   875→        return true;
   876→    }
   877→    model_matches_allowlist(model, patterns)
   878→}

   939→pub(crate) fn parse_explicit_shell_command(text: &str) -> Option<&str> {
   940→    let trimmed = text.trim_start();
   941→    let rest = trimmed.strip_prefix("/sh")?;
   942→    let first = rest.chars().next()?;
   943→    if !first.is_whitespace() {
   944→        return None;
   945→    }
   946→    let command = &rest[first.len_utf8()..];
   947→    if command.trim().is_empty() {
   948→        None
   949→    } else {
   950→        Some(command)
   951→    }
   952→}

   999→/// Detect the current user's runtime shell from environment variables.
  1000→pub(crate) fn detect_runtime_shell() -> Option<String> {
  1001→    use std::{ffi::OsStr, path::Path};
  1002→    let candidate = std::env::var("SHELL")
  1003→        .ok()
  1004→        .or_else(|| std::env::var("COMSPEC").ok())?;
  1005→    let trimmed = candidate.trim();
  1006→    if trimmed.is_empty() {
  1007→        return None;
  1008→    }
  1009→    let name = Path::new(trimmed)
  1010→        .file_name()
  1011→        .and_then(OsStr::to_str)
  1012→        .unwrap_or(trimmed)
  1013→        .trim();
  1014→    if name.is_empty() {
  1015→        None
  1016→    } else {
  1017→        Some(name.to_string())
  1018→    }
  1019→}
  1020→
  1021→pub(crate) async fn detect_host_sudo_access() -> (Option<bool>, Option<String>) {
  1022→    #[cfg(not(unix))]
  1023→    {
  1024→        return (None, Some("unsupported".to_string()));
  1025→    }
  1026→
  1027→    #[cfg(unix)]
  1028→    {
  1029→        use std::process::Stdio;
  1030→        let output = tokio::process::Command::new("sudo")
  1031→            .arg("-n")
  1032→            .arg("true")
  1033→            .stdin(Stdio::null())
  1034→            .stdout(Stdio::null())
  1035→            .stderr(Stdio::piped())
  1036→            .output()
  1037→            .await;
  1038→
  1039→        match output {
  1040→            Ok(out) if out.status.success() => (Some(true), Some("passwordless".to_string())),
  1041→            Ok(out) => {
  1042→                let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr).to_lowercase();
  1043→                if stderr.contains("a password is required") {
  1044→                    (Some(false), Some("requires_password".to_string()))
  1045→                } else if stderr.contains("not in the sudoers")
  1046→                    || stderr.contains("is not in the sudoers")
  1047→                    || stderr.contains("is not allowed to run sudo")
  1048→                    || stderr.contains("may not run sudo")
  1049→                {
  1050→                    (Some(false), Some("denied".to_string()))
  1051→                } else {
  1052→                    (None, Some("unknown".to_string()))
  1053→                }
  1054→            },
  1055→            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {
  1056→                (None, Some("not_installed".to_string()))
  1057→            },
  1058→            Err(_) => (None, Some("unknown".to_string())),
  1059→        }
  1060→    }
  1061→}

  1063→pub(crate) async fn detect_host_root_user() -> Option<bool> {
  1064→    #[cfg(not(unix))]
  1065→    {
  1066→        return None;
  1067→    }
  1068→
  1069→    #[cfg(unix)]
  1070→    {
  1071→        use std::process::Stdio;
  1072→        if let Some(uid) = std::env::var("EUID")
  1073→            .ok()
  1074→            .or_else(|| std::env::var("UID").ok())
  1075→            .and_then(|raw| raw.trim().parse::<u32>().ok())
  1076→        {
  1077→            return Some(uid == 0);
  1078→        }
  1079→        if let Ok(user) = std::env::var("USER") {
  1080→            let trimmed = user.trim();
  1081→            if !trimmed.is_empty() {
  1082→                return Some(trimmed == "root");
  1083→            }
  1084→        }
  1085→        let output = tokio::process::Command::new("id")
  1086→            .arg("-u")
  1087→            .stdin(Stdio::null())
  1088→            .stdout(Stdio::piped())
  1089→            .stderr(Stdio::null())
  1090→            .output()
  1091→            .await
  1092→            .ok()?;
  1093→        if !output.status.success() {
  1094→            return None;
  1095→        }
  1096→        let uid_text = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
  1097→        uid_text.trim().parse::<u32>().ok().map(|uid| uid == 0)
  1098→    }
  1099→}

  1127→pub(crate) fn prompt_now_for_timezone(timezone: Option<&str>) -> String {
  1128→    #[cfg(any(feature = "web-ui", feature = "push-notifications"))]
  1129→    {
  1130→        use chrono::{Local, Utc};
  1131→
  1132→        let trimmed_timezone = timezone.map(str::trim).filter(|value| !value.is_empty());
  1133→
  1134→        if let Some(tz) = trimmed_timezone.and_then(|name| name.parse::<chrono_tz::Tz>().ok()) {
  1135→            return Utc::now()
  1136→                .with_timezone(&tz)
  1137→                .format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
  1138→                .to_string();
  1139→        }
  1140→
  1141→        // Fallback to server local clock when timezone is unknown/non-IANA.
  1142→        Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z").to_string()
  1143→    }
  1144→
  1145→    #[cfg(not(any(feature = "web-ui", feature = "push-notifications")))]
  1146→    {
  1147→        let unix_secs = std::time::SystemTime::now()
  1148→            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
  1149→            .unwrap_or_default()
  1150→            .as_secs();
  1151→        let tz = timezone.unwrap_or("server-local");
  1152→        format!("unix={unix_secs} {tz}")
  1153→    }
  1154→}
  1155→
  1156→pub(crate) fn prompt_today_for_timezone(timezone: Option<&str>) -> String {
  1157→    #[cfg(any(feature = "web-ui", feature = "push-notifications"))]
  1158→    {
  1159→        use chrono::{Local, Utc};
  1160→
  1161→        let trimmed_timezone = timezone.map(str::trim).filter(|value| !value.is_empty());
  1162→
  1163→        if let Some(tz) = trimmed_timezone.and_then(|name| name.parse::<chrono_tz::Tz>().ok()) {
  1164→            return Utc::now().with_timezone(&tz).format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string();
  1165→        }
  1166→
  1167→        Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string()
  1168→    }
  1169→
  1170→    #[cfg(not(any(feature = "web-ui", feature = "push-notifications")))]
  1171→    {
  1172→        let _ = timezone;
  1173→        let unix_days = std::time::SystemTime::now()
  1174→            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
  1175→            .unwrap_or_default()
  1176→            .as_secs()
  1177→            / 86_400;
  1178→        format!("unix-day={unix_days}")
  1179→    }
  1180→}

  1270→pub(crate) fn levenshtein_distance(a: &str, b: &str) -> usize {
  1271→    if a.is_empty() {
  1272→        return b.chars().count();
  1273→    }
  1274→    if b.is_empty() {
  1275→        return a.chars().count();
  1276→    }
  1277→
  1278→    let b_chars: Vec<char> = b.chars().collect();
  1279→    let a_chars: Vec<char> = a.chars().collect();
  1280→    let mut prev: Vec<usize> = (0..=b_chars.len()).collect();
  1281→    let mut curr = vec![0; b_chars.len() + 1];
  1282→
  1283→    for (i, a_ch) in a_chars.iter().enumerate() {
  1284→        curr[0] = i + 1;
  1285→        for (j, b_ch) in b_chars.iter().enumerate() {
  1286→            let cost = usize::from(a_ch != b_ch);
  1287→            let deletion = prev[j + 1] + 1;
  1288→            let insertion = curr[j] + 1;
  1289→            let substitution = prev[j] + cost;
  1290→            curr[j + 1] = deletion.min(insertion).min(substitution);
  1291→        }
  1292→        std::mem::swap(&mut prev, &mut curr);
  1293→    }
  1294→
  1295→    prev[b_chars.len()]
  1296→}

  1298→pub(crate) fn suggest_model_ids(
  1299→    requested_model_id: &str,
  1300→    available_model_ids: &[String],
  1301→    limit: usize,
  1302→) -> Vec<String> {
  1303→    if requested_model_id.trim().is_empty() || available_model_ids.is_empty() || limit == 0 {
  1304→        return Vec::new();
  1305→    }
  1306→
  1307→    let requested_provider = model_id_provider(requested_model_id).map(str::to_ascii_lowercase);
  1308→    let requested_raw = moltis_providers::model_id::raw_model_id(requested_model_id);
  1309→    let requested_norm = normalize_model_lookup_key(requested_model_id);
  1310→    let requested_raw_norm = normalize_model_lookup_key(requested_raw);
  1311→
  1312→    let mut ranked: Vec<(String, bool, usize, usize, usize)> = available_model_ids
  1313→        .iter()
  1314→        .filter_map(|candidate| {
  1315→            let candidate_provider = model_id_provider(candidate).map(str::to_ascii_lowercase);
  1316→            let provider_match = requested_provider
  1317→                .as_deref()
  1318→                .zip(candidate_provider.as_deref())
  1319→                .is_some_and(|(left, right)| left == right);
  1320→
  1321→            let candidate_raw = moltis_providers::model_id::raw_model_id(candidate);
  1322→            let candidate_norm = normalize_model_lookup_key(candidate);
  1323→            let candidate_raw_norm = normalize_model_lookup_key(candidate_raw);
  1324→
  1325→            let raw_distance = levenshtein_distance(&requested_raw_norm, &candidate_raw_norm);
  1326→            let full_distance = levenshtein_distance(&requested_norm, &candidate_norm);
  1327→            let contains = requested_raw_norm.contains(&candidate_raw_norm)
  1328→                || candidate_raw_norm.contains(&requested_raw_norm)
  1329→                || requested_norm.contains(&candidate_norm)
  1330→                || candidate_norm.contains(&requested_raw_norm);
  1331→
  1332→            // Keep nearest neighbors and strong substring matches. This trims
  1333→            // unrelated model IDs from suggestion logs/responses.
  1334→            let distance_cap = requested_raw_norm
  1335→                .len()
  1336→                .max(candidate_raw_norm.len())
  1337→                .max(3)
  1338→                / 2
  1339→                + 2;
  1340→            if !contains && raw_distance > distance_cap {
  1341→                return None;
  1342→            }
  1343→
  1344→            Some((
  1345→                candidate.clone(),
  1346→                provider_match,
  1347→                raw_distance,
  1348→                full_distance,
  1349→                requested_raw_norm.len().abs_diff(candidate_raw_norm.len()),
  1350→            ))
  1351→        })
  1352→        .collect();
  1353→
  1354→    ranked.sort_by(|left, right| {
  1355→        right
  1356→            .1
  1357→            .cmp(&left.1) // provider match first
  1358→            .then(left.2.cmp(&right.2)) // nearest raw model id
  1359→            .then(left.3.cmp(&right.3)) // nearest full model id
  1360→            .then(left.4.cmp(&right.4)) // similar length
  1361→            .then(left.0.cmp(&right.0))
  1362→    });
  1363→
  1364→    ranked.into_iter().map(|(id, ..)| id).take(limit).collect()
  1365→}

    19→    /// Applies ONLY to sub-agents spawned via the `spawn_agent` tool. It
    20→    /// does NOT configure tool policy, model, or identity for the main

    74→impl Default for PresetMemoryConfig {
    75→    fn default() -> Self {
    76→        Self {
    77→            scope: MemoryScope::default(),
    78→            max_lines: 200,
    79→        }
    80→    }
    81→}
    82→
    83→/// Session access policy configuration for a preset.
    84→///
    85→/// Controls which sessions an agent can see and interact with via
    86→/// the `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, and `sessions_send` tools.
    87→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    88→#[serde(default)]
    89→pub struct SessionAccessPolicyConfig {
    90→    /// Only see sessions with keys matching this prefix.
    91→    pub key_prefix: Option<String>,
    92→    /// Explicit session keys this agent can access (in addition to prefix).
    93→    #[serde(default)]
    94→    pub allowed_keys: Vec<String>,
    95→    /// Whether the agent can send messages to sessions.
    96→    #[serde(default = "default_true")]
    97→    pub can_send: bool,
    98→    /// Whether the agent can access sessions from other agents.
    99→    #[serde(default)]
   100→    pub cross_agent: bool,
   101→}
   102→
   103→impl Default for SessionAccessPolicyConfig {
   104→    fn default() -> Self {
   105→        Self {
   106→            key_prefix: None,
   107→            allowed_keys: Vec::new(),
   108→            can_send: true,
   109→            cross_agent: false,
   110→        }
   111→    }
   112→}
   113→
   114→/// Spawn policy preset for sub-agents.
   115→///
   116→/// Presets allow defining specialized agent configurations that can be
   117→/// selected when spawning sub-agents. Each preset can override identity,
   118→/// model, tool policies, and system prompt.
   119→///
   120→/// **IMPORTANT:** Presets apply ONLY to sub-agents spawned via the
   121→/// `spawn_agent` tool. The `tools.allow`/`tools.deny` fields on a preset
   122→/// do NOT filter tools for the main agent session — the main session's
   123→/// tool policy is controlled by the top-level `[tools.policy]` section.
   124→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   125→#[serde(default)]
   126→pub struct AgentPreset {
   127→    /// Agent identity overrides.
   128→    pub identity: AgentIdentity,
   129→    /// Optional model override for this preset.
   130→    pub model: Option<String>,
   131→    /// Tool policy for this preset (allow/deny specific tools).
   132→    pub tools: PresetToolPolicy,
   133→    /// Restrict sub-agent to delegation/session/task tools only.
   134→    #[serde(default)]
   135→    pub delegate_only: bool,
   136→    /// Optional extra instructions appended to sub-agent system prompt.
   137→    pub system_prompt_suffix: Option<String>,
   138→    /// Maximum iterations for agent loop.
   139→    pub max_iterations: Option<u64>,
   140→    /// Timeout in seconds for the sub-agent.
   141→    pub timeout_secs: Option<u64>,
   142→    /// Session access policy for inter-agent communication.
   143→    pub sessions: Option<SessionAccessPolicyConfig>,
   144→    /// Persistent per-agent memory configuration.
   145→    pub memory: Option<PresetMemoryConfig>,
   146→    /// Reasoning/thinking effort level for models that support extended thinking.
   147→    ///
   148→    /// Controls extended thinking for models that support it (e.g. Claude Opus,
   149→    /// OpenAI o-series). Higher values enable deeper reasoning but increase
   150→    /// latency and token usage.
   151→    pub reasoning_effort: Option<ReasoningEffort>,
   152→}

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a detailed final summary report: ## Summary of Thinking Mode / Extended Reasoning Implementation Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's the complete picture of thinking mode support: --- ### 1. **Models Supporting Thinking Mode** The system supports thinking/reasoning on the following models: **Anthropic Claude:** - claude-opus-4-5+ - claude-sonnet-4-5+ - claude-opus-4-6+ - claude-sonnet-4-6+ - claude-3-7-sonnet (with extended thinking) **OpenAI:** - o-series models (o1, o1-mini, o3, o3-mini, o4, o4-mini) - GPT-5.x series (supports reasoning_effort) **Google Gemini:** - gemini-2.5+ models (Flash, Pro) - gemini-3+ models (Flash, 3.1 Pro) **Other providers:** - DeepSeek R1 / deepseek-reasoner models - xAI Grok 3+ (Grok 3, Grok 3 Mini, Grok 4 series) --- ### 2. **Core Type Definition** File: `/crates/config/src/schema.rs` (lines 34-48) ```rust #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)] #[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")] pub enum ReasoningEffort { Low, // 4096 tokens for Anthropic Medium, // 10240 tokens for Anthropic High, // 32768 tokens for Anthropic } ``` **Maps to provider-specific parameters:** - **Anthropic**: `thinking.budget_tokens` field with effort-based token budgets - **OpenAI Chat Completions API**: `reasoning_effort` field (string: "low", "medium", "high") - **OpenAI Responses API**: nested `reasoning: { "effort": "..." }` object - **Other providers**: ignored if unsupported --- ### 3. **Model Capability Detection** File: `/crates/providers/src/model_capabilities.rs` (lines 233-274) Function: `supports_reasoning_for_model(model_id: &str) -> bool` The system includes pattern-matching heuristics for all major providers: - **Anthropic**: Claude Opus/Sonnet 4.5+, 4.6+, and 3.7-Sonnet variants - **OpenAI**: o-series and GPT-5.x - **Google**: Gemini 2.5+ and 3.x - **DeepSeek**: Checks for "deepseek-r1" or "deepseek-reasoner" in model ID - **xAI Grok**: Grok 3+ and Grok 4 (Grok 2 explicitly excluded) This is exported in the `ModelCapabilities` struct: ```rust #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize)] pub struct ModelCapabilities { pub tools: bool, pub vision: bool, pub reasoning: bool, // <-- True if model supports extended thinking } ``` --- ### 4. **Configuration & Agent Setup** File: `/crates/config/src/schema/agents.rs` (lines 146-151) **Agent Presets** can configure reasoning effort per sub-agent: ```rust pub struct AgentPreset { // ... other fields ... pub reasoning_effort: Option, } ``` This allows different reasoning levels for spawned sub-agents via the `spawn_agent` tool. --- ### 5. **Provider Implementation** **Anthropic Provider:** - File: `/crates/providers/src/anthropic.rs` (lines 24-26, 98-120) - Implements `apply_thinking()` method that: - Maps `ReasoningEffort` enum to `budget_tokens` (Low=4096, Medium=10240, High=32768) - Sets `thinking: { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: N }` in request body - Auto-adjusts `max_tokens` if it's less than `budget_tokens` + 4096 **OpenAI Provider:** - File: `/crates/providers/src/openai/provider/core.rs` (lines 112-139) - Two separate methods: - `apply_reasoning_effort_chat()`: Chat Completions API → `"reasoning_effort": "low|medium|high"` - `apply_reasoning_effort_responses()`: Responses API → `"reasoning": { "effort": "..." }` - Helper: `reasoning_effort_str()` converts `ReasoningEffort` to "low"/"medium"/"high" strings Both providers implement the `LlmProvider` trait methods: - `reasoning_effort()` → returns `Option` - `with_reasoning_effort(effort)` → returns new provider instance with effort applied --- ### 6. **Model ID Suffix System** Files: - `/crates/providers/src/model_id.rs` (complete implementation) - `/crates/providers/src/registry/registration.rs` (variant generation) **Suffix Format:** `@reasoning-low|medium|high` **Examples:** - `claude-opus-4-5@reasoning-high` - `o3@reasoning-medium` - `gpt-5@reasoning-low` - `anthropic::claude-sonnet-4-5@reasoning-high` The system automatically generates reasoning variants: ```rust list_models_with_reasoning_variants() ``` For each reasoning-capable model, three additional entries are created: - `@reasoning-low` - `@reasoning-medium` - `@reasoning-high` These are parsed by `split_reasoning_suffix()` and automatically applied to provider instances at request time. --- ### 7. **Sub-Agent Reasoning via spawn_agent Tool** File: `/crates/tools/src/spawn_agent.rs` (lines 155-175) When a sub-agent is spawned: 1. The preset's `reasoning_effort` is read (if configured) 2. `provider.with_reasoning_effort(effort)` is called 3. If the provider supports the effort level, a new provider instance is created 4. If not supported, a warning is logged and the preset setting is ignored **Key behavior:** - Preset `reasoning_effort` overrides any model-ID suffix - Log warning if overriding an existing reasoning effort from model ID - Gracefully ignore if provider doesn't support it --- ### 8. **Message Content & Session Persistence** Files: - `/crates/chat/src/types.rs` (lines 140, 170) - `/crates/sessions/src/message.rs` (message serialization) **Session messages include reasoning text:** - Field name: `reasoning` (optional string) - Persisted as `serde_json::Value` in session history - Carried through compaction and history APIs **Chat broadcast payload:** - File: `/crates/chat/src/types.rs` - `reasoning: Option` field in `ChatFinalBroadcast` and `AssistantTurnOutput` --- ### 9. **Web UI Support** Files: - `/crates/web/src/templates/index.html` (thinking indicator template) - `/crates/web/src/assets/js/sessions.js` (message rendering) - `/crates/web/src/assets/js/chat-ui.js` (reasoning disclosure UI) **UI Elements:** 1. **Thinking Indicator Template** (line 369): ```html ``` 2. **Reasoning Display Function** (`appendReasoningDisclosure` in chat-ui.js): - Creates a `
` element with class `msg-reasoning` - Summary shows "Thinking..." or similar - Body contains collapsible reasoning text 3. **Message Rendering** (sessions.js, lines 728-735): - Checks for `msg.reasoning` field - Calls `appendReasoningDisclosure()` if present - Works for both streaming and non-streaming messages - Also handles reasoning in tool call outputs (line 831) --- ### 10. **Validation & Config Schema** File: `/crates/config/src/validate/schema_map.rs` The system validates: - `reasoning_effort` is only set on models that support it - Warnings logged for unsupported configurations - Graceful fallback to ignore when not supported --- ### 11. **Usage Pattern Summary** **At Agent Definition Time:** ```toml [agents.presets.research] reasoning_effort = "high" # Sub-agents spawned with this preset use high reasoning ``` **At Model Selection Time:** ``` claude-opus-4-5@reasoning-high # Request high reasoning for this model ``` **Provider Behavior:** 1. `get_model()` looks up the model ID 2. `split_reasoning_suffix()` extracts effort level if present 3. `provider.with_reasoning_effort(effort)` creates new provider with effort 4. During request building, `apply_thinking()` / `apply_reasoning_effort_*()` embeds the effort in the API payload 5. Response processing extracts reasoning text to `msg.reasoning` 6. Web UI displays reasoning in collapsible disclosure --- ### Key Files to Know: | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `crates/config/src/schema.rs` | `ReasoningEffort` enum definition | | `crates/config/src/schema/agents.rs` | Agent preset config with `reasoning_effort` | | `crates/providers/src/model_capabilities.rs` | Model capability detection, `supports_reasoning_for_model()` | | `crates/providers/src/model_id.rs` | Suffix parsing: `split_reasoning_suffix()` | | `crates/providers/src/anthropic.rs` | Anthropic `thinking` parameter implementation | | `crates/providers/src/openai/provider/core.rs` | OpenAI `reasoning_effort` / `reasoning` parameter implementation | | `crates/tools/src/spawn_agent.rs` | Sub-agent reasoning configuration | | `crates/chat/src/types.rs` | Reasoning field in chat messages and broadcasts | | `crates/web/src/assets/js/sessions.js` | Message rendering with reasoning | | `crates/web/src/assets/js/chat-ui.js` | `appendReasoningDisclosure()` UI function |

content.text:
Great, now I have the full picture. Here's my analysis:

## Issue #739: Configuring Reasoning Effort for OpenRouter

The user wants to set reasoning effort for OpenRouter models — either globally or per-model. Currently, reasoning effort is only applied for **native** providers (Anthropic, OpenAI) but not for BYOM (bring-your-own-model) providers like OpenRouter.

## Current State of Thinking Mode Support

The codebase already has solid reasoning infrastructure:

**`ReasoningEffort` enum** (`crates/config/src/schema.rs`): `Low`, `Medium`, `High`

**Models with thinking support** (detected via `supports_reasoning_for_model()` in `model_capabilities.rs`):

| Provider | Models |
|----------|--------|
| **Anthropic** | Claude Opus 4.5+, Sonnet 4.5+, Opus 4.6+, Sonnet 4.6+, 3.7 Sonnet |
| **OpenAI** | o1, o1-mini, o3, o3-mini, o4, o4-mini, GPT-5.x |
| **Google** | Gemini 2.5+, 3.x Flash/Pro |
| **DeepSeek** | R1 / deepseek-reasoner |
| **xAI** | Grok 3+, Grok 4 series |

**Existing mechanisms:**
- Model ID suffix: `claude-opus-4-5@reasoning-high` — auto-generates 3 variants per reasoning-capable model
- Agent presets: `reasoning_effort = "high"` in TOML config
- Web UI already renders reasoning output in collapsible `<details>` disclosures

## Would a "Brain" Toggle in the Web UI Make Sense?

**Yes, absolutely.** Here's why:

1. **The backend is ready** — `ReasoningEffort` enum, `with_reasoning_effort()` on providers, and `ModelCapabilities.reasoning` flag all exist. The web UI just doesn't expose a control for it yet.

2. **The `@reasoning-*` suffix approach is awkward for users** — it triples the model list (every reasoning-capable model gets 3 extra entries). A toggle next to the model selector would be much cleaner.

3. **It solves #739 naturally** — OpenRouter proxies models from multiple providers. If the UI lets users pick reasoning effort for any model flagged as reasoning-capable, it works regardless of whether the backend is native Anthropic or OpenRouter-proxied Claude.

4. **Implementation path is clear:**
   - The `/api/gon` endpoint already sends `ModelCapabilities` (including `reasoning: bool`) to the frontend
   - When a reasoning-capable model is selected, show a brain icon / toggle with Low/Medium/High options
   - Send the chosen effort as part of the chat request (could be a simple query param or body field)
   - Backend already knows how to apply it via `with_reasoning_effort()`

5. **Different providers use different mechanisms** but that's already abstracted — Anthropic uses `budget_tokens`, OpenAI uses `reasoning_effort` string, and the provider layer handles the translation.

**The main gap for #739 specifically** is that OpenRouter (and other OpenAI-compatible providers) need to actually pass the reasoning parameters through. The `apply_reasoning_effort_chat()` / `apply_reasoning_effort_responses()` methods on the OpenAI provider would need to be used by OpenRouter's provider implementation too, since OpenRouter supports the `reasoning_effort` field for compatible models.

**Suggested approach:** A small brain icon next to the model selector that, when the selected model has `reasoning: true`, expands to show Low/Medium/High buttons (similar to selection cards already used elsewhere). This would be cleaner than the current suffix-based model variants and would address #739 for all BYOM providers at once.

input.prompt:
Plan the implementation of a "brain" toggle in the web UI for reasoning effort control. This should appear next to the model/provider selector and show Low/Medium/High options when the selected model supports reasoning.

Context from codebase exploration:

**Backend (already exists):**
- `ReasoningEffort` enum in `crates/config/src/schema.rs`: Low, Medium, High
- `ModelCapabilities { tools, vision, reasoning }` in `crates/providers/src/model_capabilities.rs`
- `supports_reasoning_for_model(model_id)` function for detection
- `with_reasoning_effort(effort)` on providers
- Model ID suffix system: `model@reasoning-high` auto-generates variants
- `/api/gon` endpoint sends model capabilities to frontend
- Agent presets already support `reasoning_effort` in TOML

**Frontend (current state):**
- Web UI in `crates/web/src/assets/` (JS, CSS, HTML)
- `index.html` has the main layout including model/provider selection
- `chat-ui.js` handles chat UI rendering
- `sessions.js` handles message rendering (already renders reasoning output in collapsible details)
- Uses Preact/HTM for some components, vanilla JS for others
- Selection cards pattern (`.model-card`, `.backend-card`) for option selection
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- `gon.js` for server-injected data
- `events.js` for WebSocket event bus

**What needs to happen:**
1. Frontend: Add a brain toggle/control next to the model selector that shows reasoning effort options (Low/Medium/High) when the selected model has reasoning capability
2. Backend: Ensure the reasoning effort selection from the UI is sent with chat requests and applied to the provider
3. OpenRouter and other BYOM providers need to pass reasoning_effort through (this may already work if they use the OpenAI-compatible path)

**Rules from CLAUDE.md:**
- Avoid creating HTML from JS — add hidden elements in index.html, toggle visibility
- Always use Tailwind classes instead of inline styles
- Reuse CSS classes from components.css
- Use selection cards pattern for option selection
- Server events via WebSocket event bus, not window.addEventListener
- gon pattern for server-injected data
- Each UI tab gets its own API namespace

Please identify all files that need changes, the order of implementation, and any risks or considerations. Focus on a clean, minimal implementation.

Now I have a complete picture of the architecture. Let me formulate the full implementation plan. --- ## Implementation Plan: Reasoning Effort "Brain" Toggle ### Overview The goal is to add a reasoning effort control in the chat toolbar (next to the model selector) that allows users to select Low/Medium/High reasoning when the currently selected model supports extended thinking. The design leverages the existing `@reasoning-low/medium/high` model ID suffix system already built into the backend. ### Architecture Decision: Frontend-Only with Model ID Suffix The cleanest approach is to handle reasoning effort entirely on the frontend by appending the `@reasoning-*` suffix to the model ID before sending to the backend. This is the correct approach because: 1. The backend already handles `@reasoning-low/medium/high` suffixes transparently in `ProviderRegistry::get()` (at `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/providers/src/registry/registration.rs`, line 1068-1088). 2. The `models.list` RPC already returns `supportsReasoning: true/false` for each model (at `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/chat/src/models.rs`, line 595). 3. The `chat.send` RPC accepts a `model` param that flows directly to `reg.get(id)` (at `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/chat/src/service/chat_impl/send.rs`, line 498-501). 4. **No backend changes are required.** The entire feature can be implemented purely in frontend JS and CSS. The only question is whether to continue showing `@reasoning-*` variants as separate items in the model dropdown (current behavior) or to filter them out and use a dedicated toggle instead. The toggle approach is much better UX, so the plan filters reasoning variants from the model dropdown. ### Implementation Steps (Ordered) #### Step 1: Add Reasoning Effort State **File: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/stores/model-store.js`** Add a signal for the selected reasoning effort and a computed signal for whether the current model supports reasoning: - `reasoningEffort` signal: `signal(localStorage.getItem("moltis-reasoning-effort") || "")` -- empty string means "off" (no reasoning suffix). - `supportsReasoning` computed signal: derives from `selectedModel.value?.supportsReasoning`. - `effectiveModelId` computed signal: combines `selectedModelId` with `@reasoning-{effort}` suffix when effort is set and model supports reasoning. - `setReasoningEffort(effort)` function: updates the signal and persists to `localStorage`. Export these on the `modelStore` object. #### Step 2: Filter Reasoning Variants from Model Dropdown **File: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/models.js`** In `renderModelList()` (line 100-128), add a filter to exclude model IDs containing `@reasoning-` from the dropdown list. These virtual models are now replaced by the dedicated toggle. The filter goes at line 105 inside the existing filter chain: ```js var filtered = allModels.filter((m) => { // Hide reasoning variants — the brain toggle handles these now. if (m.id.indexOf("@reasoning-") !== -1) return false; // ... existing search filter }); ``` #### Step 3: Update `applySelectedModelToChatParams` to Use Effective Model ID **File: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/page-chat.js`** Modify `applySelectedModelToChatParams()` (line 1159-1164) to use the effective model ID (with reasoning suffix) instead of the raw selected model ID: ```js function applySelectedModelToChatParams(chatParams) { var effectiveId = modelStore.effectiveModelId.value; if (!effectiveId) return; chatParams.model = effectiveId; setSessionModel(S.activeSessionKey, effectiveId); } ``` Import `modelStore` at the top of the file (already imported via `models.js` dependency chain). #### Step 4: Add the Brain Toggle HTML in the Chat Toolbar **File: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/page-chat.js`** In the `chatPageHTML` string (line 1289-1395), add a reasoning effort combo right after the `#modelCombo` div and before the `#nodeCombo` div. The HTML follows the same `model-combo` pattern used by the model and node selectors: ```html
``` This uses `icon-sparkles` (available in `input.css`) and reuses the existing `.model-combo` / `.model-dropdown` CSS classes entirely. #### Step 5: Wire Up the Brain Toggle Logic **File: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/page-chat.js`** Add functions to manage the reasoning toggle (similar to `bindModelComboEvents` / `bindNodeComboEvents` patterns): 1. `renderReasoningOptions()` -- renders the four options (Off, Low, Medium, High) using the `.model-dropdown-item` pattern. 2. `selectReasoningEffort(effort)` -- calls `modelStore.setReasoningEffort(effort)`, updates label, closes dropdown. 3. `openReasoningDropdown()` / `closeReasoningDropdown()` -- show/hide. 4. `bindReasoningComboEvents()` -- attach click/keydown/outside-click handlers. 5. `updateReasoningComboVisibility()` -- show the combo only when the selected model has `supportsReasoning === true`. Use a Preact `effect()` on `modelStore.supportsReasoning` to reactively toggle visibility. In `initializeChatControls()` (line 1580-1607), add initialization of the reasoning combo elements and call `bindReasoningComboEvents()`. In the teardown function (line 1722-1758), clean up the reasoning combo references. #### Step 6: Reset Reasoning on Model Change **File: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/models.js`** In `selectModel()` (line 36-46), after selecting a new model, check if the new model supports reasoning. If not, reset `modelStore.setReasoningEffort("")` so the suffix is cleared. If it does support reasoning, keep the current effort. This prevents stale reasoning suffixes from being sent for non-reasoning models. #### Step 7: Add i18n Strings **Files:** - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/locales/en/chat.js` - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/locales/fr/chat.js` - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/locales/zh/chat.js` Add keys: `reasoning.off`, `reasoning.low`, `reasoning.medium`, `reasoning.high`, `reasoning.title` ("Reasoning effort"). #### Step 8: Add E2E Tests **File: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/reasoning-toggle.spec.js` (new file)** Tests to add: 1. Reasoning combo hidden when a non-reasoning model is selected. 2. Reasoning combo visible when a reasoning-capable model is selected. 3. Clicking the toggle opens the dropdown with Off/Low/Medium/High options. 4. Selecting an effort level updates the label and closes the dropdown. 5. Reasoning effort persists across page reload (localStorage). 6. Model ID suffix `@reasoning-*` is included in chat.send params. 7. Switching to a non-reasoning model resets the toggle to "Off". 8. Reasoning variants (`@reasoning-*`) do not appear in the model dropdown. ### Risks and Considerations 1. **Model ID suffix in session metadata**: When the user selects reasoning effort, the effective model ID (with `@reasoning-*` suffix) is stored in session metadata via `sessions.patch`. This means the reasoning level is persisted with the session. On session reload, the model will appear as e.g. `claude-opus-4-5@reasoning-high`. The reasoning combo needs to parse this on session switch to show the correct state. The `switchSession` path should check if the current session model has a reasoning suffix and set the toggle accordingly. 2. **Model label in toolbar**: The model combo label shows the model display name. If the session was previously set with `@reasoning-high`, the model store needs to strip the suffix to find the base model for display, or the resolved model won't match. 3. **File size limit**: `page-chat.js` is already 1758 lines, close to the 1500-line CI limit. The new reasoning toggle code should ideally be extracted into a separate `reasoning-toggle.js` module (similar to `sandbox.js`, `voice-input.js`) to stay within limits and follow the codebase pattern. 4. **Mobile responsiveness**: The toolbar already handles wrapping (flex row). The reasoning combo uses the same `model-combo` pattern which is already responsive. No special mobile handling needed beyond what exists. 5. **Tailwind rebuild**: After any CSS class changes, run `cd crates/web/ui && npx tailwindcss -i input.css -o ../src/assets/style.css --minify`. 6. **No backend changes needed**: The `@reasoning-*` suffix system in `ProviderRegistry::get()` already handles everything. The model list already includes `supportsReasoning` in the JSON payload. Zero Rust code changes. ### Suggested Module Split To keep `page-chat.js` under the 1500-line limit, create a new file: **`/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/reasoning-toggle.js`** This file would contain: - `renderReasoningOptions()` - `selectReasoningEffort(effort)` - `openReasoningDropdown()` / `closeReasoningDropdown()` - `bindReasoningComboEvents()` - `updateReasoningComboVisibility()` - `parseReasoningFromModelId(modelId)` -- returns `{ baseId, effort }` for session restore - `unbindReasoningEvents()` -- for teardown Then `page-chat.js` would just import and call `bindReasoningComboEvents()` in init and `unbindReasoningEvents()` in teardown. ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/stores/model-store.js` - Add reasoning effort signal, effectiveModelId computed, supportsReasoning computed - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/page-chat.js` - Add reasoning combo HTML to chatPageHTML, wire init/teardown, modify applySelectedModelToChatParams - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/models.js` - Filter `@reasoning-*` variants from dropdown, reset reasoning on model change - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/web/src/assets/js/reasoning-toggle.js` (new) - Reasoning toggle combo logic, extracted for file-size compliance - `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/literate-coral/crates/providers/src/registry/registration.rs` - No changes needed, but reference for understanding `@reasoning-*` suffix resolution (lines 1068-1088, 1117-1146)

toolUseResult.prompt:
Plan the implementation of a "brain" toggle in the web UI for reasoning effort control. This should appear next to the model/provider selector and show Low/Medium/High options when the selected model supports reasoning.

Context from codebase exploration:

**Backend (already exists):**
- `ReasoningEffort` enum in `crates/config/src/schema.rs`: Low, Medium, High
- `ModelCapabilities { tools, vision, reasoning }` in `crates/providers/src/model_capabilities.rs`
- `supports_reasoning_for_model(model_id)` function for detection
- `with_reasoning_effort(effort)` on providers
- Model ID suffix system: `model@reasoning-high` auto-generates variants
- `/api/gon` endpoint sends model capabilities to frontend
- Agent presets already support `reasoning_effort` in TOML

**Frontend (current state):**
- Web UI in `crates/web/src/assets/` (JS, CSS, HTML)
- `index.html` has the main layout including model/provider selection
- `chat-ui.js` handles chat UI rendering
- `sessions.js` handles message rendering (already renders reasoning output in collapsible details)
- Uses Preact/HTM for some components, vanilla JS for others
- Selection cards pattern (`.model-card`, `.backend-card`) for option selection
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- `gon.js` for server-injected data
- `events.js` for WebSocket event bus

**What needs to happen:**
1. Frontend: Add a brain toggle/control next to the model selector that shows reasoning effort options (Low/Medium/High) when the selected model has reasoning capability
2. Backend: Ensure the reasoning effort selection from the UI is sent with chat requests and applied to the provider
3. OpenRouter and other BYOM providers need to pass reasoning_effort through (this may already work if they use the OpenAI-compatible path)

**Rules from CLAUDE.md:**
- Avoid creating HTML from JS — add hidden elements in index.html, toggle visibility
- Always use Tailwind classes instead of inline styles
- Reuse CSS classes from components.css
- Use selection cards pattern for option selection
- Server events via WebSocket event bus, not window.addEventListener
- gon pattern for server-injected data
- Each UI tab gets its own API namespace

Please identify all files that need changes, the order of implementation, and any risks or considerations. Focus on a clean, minimal implementation.

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. The model list API already sends reasoning variants with display names like "Claude Opus 4.5 (high reasoning)". The approach is clear:

1. **Filter out `@reasoning-*` variants** from the model dropdown (the toggle replaces them)
2. **Add reasoning effort signals** to the model store
3. **Create a `reasoning-toggle.js`** module for the toggle UI
4. **Modify `page-chat.js`** to include the toggle in the toolbar and wire it up
5. **Handle session restore** — parse `@reasoning-*` suffix from stored model IDs

Let me implement this now.

+  return { baseId: modelId.substring(0, idx), effort: modelId.substring(idx + REASONING_SEP.length) };

// ── Chat UI Chinese (Simplified) strings ────────────────────

export default {
	// ── Input area ───────────────────────────────────────────
	placeholder: "输入消息...",
	sendBtn: "发送",
	micTooltip: "点击开始录音",
	micStarting: "启动麦克风...",
	micStopAndSend: "点击停止并发送",
	voiceTranscribing: "转录中...",
	voiceTranscribingMessage: "正在转录语音...",

	// ── Slash commands ───────────────────────────────────────
	slashClear: "清除对话历史",
	slashCompact: "压缩对话以节省 token",
	slashContext: "显示会话上下文和项目信息",

	// ── Message labels ───────────────────────────────────────
	viaChannel: "通过 {{label}}",
	provider: "供应商：{{name}}",
	allowed: "已允许",
	denied: "已拒绝",
	expired: "已过期",
	queued: "排队中",

	// ── Sandbox / MCP labels ─────────────────────────────────
	sandboxed: "沙盒",
	sandboxDirect: "直连",
	sandboxDisabled: "已禁用",
	sandboxUnavailable: "不可用",
	sandboxDisabledHint:
		"在没有容器运行时的云部署中，沙盒功能已禁用。请在装有 Docker 或 Apple Container 的虚拟机上安装以启用此功能。",
	sandboxToggleTooltip: "切换沙盒模式",
	sandboxImageTooltip: "沙盒镜像",
	mcpEnabled: "MCP",
	mcpEnabledTooltip: "MCP 工具已启用 \u2014 点击为此会话禁用",
	mcpDisabled: "MCP 已关闭",
	mcpDisabledTooltip: "MCP 工具已禁用 \u2014 点击为此会话启用",
	mcpToggleTooltip: "切换此会话的 MCP 工具",

	// ── Debug panel ──────────────────────────────────────────
	debugTooltip: "显示上下文调试信息",
	debugLabel: "调试",
	contextTooltip: "显示完整 LLM 上下文（系统提示 + 历史记录）",
	contextLabel: "上下文",

content.text:
Here's a summary of all changes:

## Changes Summary

### New files (2)
- **`crates/web/src/assets/js/reasoning-toggle.js`** — Self-contained reasoning toggle module with bind/unbind lifecycle, dropdown rendering, and session restore helper
- **`crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/reasoning-toggle.spec.js`** — 7 E2E tests covering visibility, dropdown options, effort selection, model ID suffix, variant filtering, and auto-reset

### Modified files (7)

**`stores/model-store.js`** — Added:
- `reasoningEffort` signal (persisted to localStorage)
- `supportsReasoning` computed (derives from selected model)
- `effectiveModelId` computed (appends `@reasoning-*` suffix when active)
- `parseReasoningSuffix()` / `isReasoningVariant()` helpers
- `setReasoningEffort()` method
- On fetch, strips reasoning suffix from saved model ID and restores effort

**`models.js`** — Filters out `@reasoning-*` virtual variants from the model dropdown (1 line)

**`page-chat.js`** — Added:
- Reasoning combo HTML in toolbar (between model and node combos)
- `bindReasoningToggle()` in init, `unbindReasoningToggle()` in teardown
- `applySelectedModelToChatParams()` now uses `modelStore.effectiveModelId` instead of `S.selectedModelId`

**`sessions.js`** — `restoreSessionState()` now calls `restoreReasoningFromModelId()` to strip `@reasoning-*` suffix from stored model IDs and restore the toggle state

**Locale files** (en/fr/zh) — Added `reasoningTooltip`, `reasoningOff/Low/Medium/High` strings

### Zero backend changes
The feature leverages the existing `@reasoning-*` model ID suffix system — the backend already resolves these to the correct provider with reasoning effort applied.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(web): add reasoning effort toggle to chat toolbar

Add a "brain" toggle next to the model selector that lets users pick
Off/Low/Medium/High reasoning effort for models that support extended
thinking. The toggle reuses the existing @reasoning-* model ID suffix
system — no backend changes required.

When a reasoning-capable model is selected, a sparkles icon combo
appears in the toolbar. Selecting an effort level appends the
corresponding @reasoning-low/medium/high suffix to the model ID sent
via chat.send. The toggle auto-hides for non-reasoning models and
resets the effort when switching away.

The @reasoning-* virtual model variants are now filtered from the
model dropdown since the toggle replaces them with a cleaner UX.
Session restore correctly parses stored model IDs with reasoning
suffixes to restore the toggle state.

Closes #739
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat(web): add reasoning effort toggle to chat toolbar" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Adds a reasoning effort toggle (sparkles icon) to the chat toolbar, visible when the selected model supports extended thinking
- Users can pick Off/Low/Medium/High — the selected effort is appended as a `@reasoning-*` suffix to the model ID sent to the backend
- Filters `@reasoning-*` virtual model variants from the model dropdown (the toggle replaces them)
- Handles session restore: parses stored model IDs with reasoning suffixes to restore toggle state
- Zero backend changes — leverages the existing `@reasoning-*` suffix resolution in `ProviderRegistry::get()`

Closes #739

## Validation

### Completed

- [x] `biome check --write` passes on all changed JS files
- [x] `cargo check -p moltis-web` compiles successfully
- [x] Tailwind CSS rebuilt
- [x] E2E tests written (`reasoning-toggle.spec.js`)

### Remaining

- [ ] `npx playwright test e2e/specs/reasoning-toggle.spec.js` — E2E tests pass
- [ ] `just lint` — clippy passes
- [ ] `just test` — all Rust tests pass
- [ ] Manual QA with a live reasoning-capable model (Claude Opus 4.5, o3, etc.)

## Manual QA

1. Open chat, select a reasoning-capable model (e.g. Claude Opus 4.5) — sparkles toggle should appear
2. Click the toggle — dropdown shows Off/Low/Medium/High
3. Select "High" — label updates, dropdown closes
4. Send a message — verify the model ID in the request includes `@reasoning-high` suffix
5. Switch to a non-reasoning model (e.g. GPT-4o) — toggle should hide, effort resets to Off
6. Switch to a session that was previously using `@reasoning-high` — toggle should restore to "High"
7. Verify `@reasoning-*` variants no longer appear as separate entries in the model dropdown
EOF
)"

content.text:
So the compiled style.css uses relative paths `./icons/masks/` relative to the CSS output dir (`src/assets/css/`), and the icon masks live at `src/assets/icons/masks/`. The `input.css` path `./icons/masks/` works because Tailwind resolves URLs from the output path. The brain SVG is already in the right place.

Now I need to also put the brain SVG in the `ui/icons/masks/` relative path for Tailwind compilation. Actually, looking at the output CSS, it keeps `./icons/masks/` relative to the output. The output is at `src/assets/css/style.css` and the masks are at `src/assets/icons/masks/` — so `./icons/masks/mask-brain.svg` from `src/assets/css/` would resolve to `src/assets/icons/masks/mask-brain.svg`. That's exactly where I put it.

But `input.css` uses the same `./icons/masks/` path, and it's at `ui/input.css`. During Tailwind build, the URL is resolved relative to the output, not the input. Let me verify by rebuilding.

content.text: Let me fix the font size in the reasoning dropdown to match the model dropdown, then push both commits.

The reasoning dropdown items use `.model-dropdown-item` which inherits the same styles as the model dropdown. The issue is likely the combo button label itself. Let me check the CSS for the combo button.

The `.model-combo-btn` has `font-size: 0.78rem` (line 1098). The model combo label gets the same style. The reasoning combo uses the same class, so the button font size should be the same. But the `#reasoningComboLabel` text ("Off"/"Low"/"Medium"/"High") gets that same font size.

The issue could be that the model combo button text is truncated/constrained by `max-width: 220px` while the reasoning label has no constraint and the overall button may render larger. Or possibly the dropdown items have a different appearance. Let me check the `model-dropdown-item` font size.

The `.model-dropdown-item` doesn't set a font-size itself — it inherits from the parent. The model items inside it have `.model-item-label` at `0.78rem`. But the reasoning dropdown items just use `textContent` directly on the `.model-dropdown-item` div without a `.model-item-label` wrapper, so they inherit the default body font size which is likely larger.

The fix is to match the font size on the reasoning dropdown items.
